title: vSAN Witness Appliance Health
exclusions: Boilerplate `vsan:witness` in older templates is not a real Splunk sourcetype; production JSON lines should land as `vmware:vsan:witness` from a `pyVmomi` or PowerCLI poller. This file does not replace UC-19.2.17 (vSAN data-host disk group health), UC-19.2.27 (vSAN free-space and capacity on data stores), or UC-19.2.30 (component resync and rebuild backlog on data hosts). It does not cover vSphere host-cluster HA admission control for generic VMs, vCenter Server HA (the management-plane feature), or guest-cluster quorum inside a guest OS. Event-only `vmware:vc:event` streams are listed as a gap layer, not the primary quantitative feed. AHCI, Nutanix, or open-source HCI stacks are different APIs entirely.
evidence: Saved search `uc_19_2_23_vsan_witness_appliance_health` in the HCI monitoring app, HEC index `vmware` or `hci` with 13-month online retention, alert history, and the versioned `vsan_witness_owner.csv` stored beside Splunk with change tickets for freeze edits.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab stretched cluster, stop the witness appliance management interface for five minutes; within two poll cycles the saved search should yield `severity_bucket` of `high` or `critical` (depending on how `quorum` and `vsan_object_health` react), and a deliberately forced `quorum=not_met` in fixture JSON should always surface as `critical` even when `change_freeze_until` is in the future.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an approved change, set `change_freeze_until` in `vsan_witness_owner.csv` to a future time that includes the test window, reload the lookup, and confirm a synthetic `low` or `medium` from stale heartbeat is coerced to `ok` while a lab-injected `critical` quorum loss still shows `critical` and pages.

dataSources: Primary: `(index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype=vmware:vsan:witness` â€” JSON from a custom HEC line produced by a Python `pyVmomi` or vSphere REST poller (service account) that calls `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/witness` and related stretched-cluster health on vCenter 7.0 U2+ (or normalises SOAP `vsanClusterGetConfig` / `VsanQuerySpaceUsage` style responses on older builds). One event per stretched cluster per poll (60â€“120s). Fields use snake_case in Splunk with optional camelCase copies from the REST payload: `witness_host` â†” `witnessHost`, `witness_state`, `connection_state` â†” `connectionState`, `fault_domain_count` â†” `faultDomainCount`, `preferred_fd` â†” `preferredFd`, `last_witness_heartbeat`, `shared_witness_id`, `serving_clusters` (8.0 U2+ shared witness). Companion optional stream: `vmware:vsan:health` for `vsanClusterQueryHealthSummary` rollups. Gap-filling: `vmware:vc:event` from the Splunk Add-on for VMware filtered to `esx.problem.vob.vsan.witness.*` and `com.vmware.vc.HA.WitnessHostNotFound` (sparse, not a substitute for the poll). The boilerplate name `vsan:witness` is not a real sourcetype; production convention is `vmware:vsan:witness` from a poller you operate.
app: No first-party Splunkbase TA ships `vmware:vsan:witness`. Combine: (1) Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 3215) for `vmware:vc:event` only if you need the gap path; (2) vSphere Automation SDK or `pyVmomi` on a Heavy Forwarder with HEC; (3) Windows PowerShell with VMware PowerCLI 13+ piped to HEC. Reference: HEC in Splunk docs, `https://github.com/vmware/pyvmomi`, VMware vSphere API REST for vSAN cluster witness.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.23 â€” vSAN Witness Appliance Health: (cluster, witness_host) severity from vmware:vsan:witness with heartbeat age, fault-domain count, change-freeze (non-critical). Boilerplate vsan:witness is not a real indexable sourcetype.")`
(index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:witness" earliest=-1h@h
| eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, ""))
| eval witness_host=toString(coalesce(witness_host, witnessHost, ""))
| eval witness_state_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_state, witnessState, ""))
| eval connection_state_raw=toString(coalesce(connection_state, connectionState, ""))
| eval fault_domain_count_raw=tonumber(toString(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, "-1")), 10)
| eval preferred_fd=toString(coalesce(preferred_fd, preferredFd, ""))
| eval secondary_fd=toString(coalesce(secondary_fd, secondaryFd, ""))
| eval witness_fd=toString(coalesce(witness_fd, witnessFd, ""))
| eval witness_disk_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_disk_health, witnessDiskHealth, ""))
| eval witness_host_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_host_health, witnessHostHealth, ""))
| eval quorum_raw=toString(coalesce(quorum, ""))
| eval voh_raw=toString(coalesce(vsan_object_health, vsanObjectHealth, ""))
| eval last_hb=toString(coalesce(last_witness_heartbeat, lastWitnessHeartbeat, ""))
| eval shared_witness_id=toString(coalesce(shared_witness_id, sharedWitnessId, ""))
| eval serving_clusters=coalesce(serving_clusters, servingClusters, "")
| stats max(_time) AS _time
    values(vcenter) AS vcenters
    latest(witness_state_raw) AS witness_state_l
    latest(connection_state_raw) AS connection_state_l
    latest(fault_domain_count_raw) AS fault_domain_count_l
    latest(preferred_fd) AS preferred_fd
    latest(secondary_fd) AS secondary_fd
    latest(witness_fd) AS witness_fd
    latest(witness_disk_raw) AS witness_disk_l
    latest(witness_host_raw) AS witness_host_l
    latest(quorum_raw) AS quorum_l
    latest(voh_raw) AS voh_l
    latest(last_hb) AS last_witness_heartbeat_l
    latest(shared_witness_id) AS shared_witness_id
    latest(serving_clusters) AS serving_clusters
  BY cluster, witness_host
| eval vcenter=if(mvcount(vcenters)=1, mvindex(vcenters, 0), mvjoin(vcenters, ", "))
| eval witness_state=lower(trim(witness_state_l))
| eval connection_state=lower(trim(connection_state_l))
| eval quorum=lower(trim(quorum_l))
| eval fault_domain_count=if(fault_domain_count_l>=0, fault_domain_count_l, null())
| eval witness_disk_health=lower(trim(witness_disk_l))
| eval witness_host_health=lower(trim(witness_host_l))
| eval vsan_object_health=trim(voh_l)
| eval last_witness_heartbeat=last_witness_heartbeat_l
| eval hb_epoch=coalesce(
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N")
  )
| eval heartbeat_age=if(isnotnull(hb_epoch), now()-hb_epoch, null())
| eval crit_q=(quorum!="met")
| eval crit_w=(witness_state="disconnected" OR witness_state="notfound")
| eval crit_v=(vsan_object_health="Inaccessible")
| eval high_d=(witness_disk_health="unhealthy")
| eval high_hh=(witness_host_health="error")
| eval high_v=(vsan_object_health="Reduced-Availability-No-Rebuild")
| eval med_w=(witness_state="notresponding")
| eval med_hh=(witness_host_health="warning")
| eval med_v=(vsan_object_health="Reduced-Availability-With-Rebuild")
| eval low_hb=((isnotnull(heartbeat_age)) AND (heartbeat_age>300))
| eval low_fd=((isnotnull(fault_domain_count)) AND (fault_domain_count!=3))
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    (crit_q=1) OR (crit_w=1) OR (crit_v=1), "critical",
    (high_d=1) OR (high_hh=1) OR (high_v=1), "high",
    (med_w=1) OR (med_hh=1) OR (med_v=1), "medium",
    (low_hb=1) OR (low_fd=1), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| lookup vsan_witness_owner cluster OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, is_shared_witness
| eval freeze_active=if(
    isnotnull(change_freeze_until)
    AND (len(toString(change_freeze_until))>0)
    AND (now()<coalesce(strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%d"), 0)),
    1, 0
  )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical"), "ok", severity_bucket)
| where severity_bucket!="ok"
| eval sev_order=case(
    severity_bucket="critical", 1,
    severity_bucket="high", 2,
    severity_bucket="medium", 3,
    severity_bucket="low", 4,
    1=1, 9
  )
| sort 0 sev_order, cluster, witness_host
| table _time, severity_bucket, sev_order, cluster, witness_host, vcenter, quorum, witness_state, connection_state, fault_domain_count, vsan_object_health, witness_disk_health, witness_host_health, heartbeat_age, last_witness_heartbeat, shared_witness_id, serving_clusters, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active, is_shared_witness, crit_q, crit_w, crit_v, high_d, high_hh, high_v, med_w, med_hh, med_v, low_hb, low_fd
description: Observes each stretched vSAN clusterâ€™s dedicated witness at `vmware:vsan:witness` by rolling up the latest poll per `(cluster, witness_host)` and evaluating tie-breaker signals: `quorum` not `met`, `witness_state` in `disconnected` or `notFound`, per-site `vsan_object_health` including `Inaccessible`, `witness_disk_health` on the metadata-only side, and `witness_host_health`, plus a derived `heartbeat_age` from `last_witness_heartbeat` with ISO-8601 variants, and a guard when `fault_domain_count` is not three. The search coalesces snake_case and camelCase field names, assigns a five-tier `severity_bucket` (critical through low), enriches with `vsan_witness_owner` for service ownership, and applies a `change_freeze_until` gate that mutes only non-critical noise during approved windows. Optional `shared_witness_id` and `serving_clusters` are preserved for vSphere 8.0 U2+ shared-witness topologies. This is not a per-data-host disk-group or capacity signal; it is the separate small footprint that only participates in site-partition decisions.
value: If the third-site witness is unhealthy long enough, everyday stretched-cluster work can continue, yet the next time the two data sites cannot see each other the clusterâ€™s tie-breaker math may be unsafe: operators risk split-brain on storage, awkward forced failovers, or a recovery path that needs hands-on work instead of clean automation. Early visibility keeps storage and platform teams ahead of a wide-area blip, planned router work, or certificate rotation on the witness, preserves consistent paging when quorum truly drops, and avoids noisy pages during controlled freezes while still allowing a genuine critical quorum loss to break through. Insurance for application owners: fewer surprise dual-writes or inconsistent states after inter-site network events.
implementation: Provision HEC to `index=vmware` or `index=hci`, deploy the poller that emits `vmware:vsan:witness` once per cluster per minute, add `lookups/vsan_witness_owner.csv` with `change_freeze_until`, schedule this saved search every 5â€“10 minutes on `earliest=-1h@h`, wire alert actions with throttle on `cluster`+`witness_host` for 30â€“60 minutes, and keep a non-suppressed sister report for trend review.
visualization: Row 1: two single values for `critical` and `high` `severity_bucket` counts. Row 2: timechart of poll volume by `cluster` to catch HEC loss. Row 3: table mirroring the final `table` with colour rules on `severity_bucket`. Row 4: sparkline of `heartbeat_age` for the top noisy witnesses. Drilldown: open vSphere client to the same cluster and witness host context your runbook names.

references.title: VMware vSphere â€” Stretched Clusters and Witness Hosts (documentation hub)
Splunk â€” Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: Witness `witness_state=disconnected` for under two minutes during an OVA guest reboot for guest-OS security patching is usually benign; file a 120s rolling minimum before paging when your change record shows maintenance. A planned multi-hour WAN or MPLS work between data sites can leave `quorum=met` while a witness site is temporarily unreachable; align `change_freeze_until` in `vsan_witness_owner.csv` with the networking CAB window. Right after a brand-new witness deploy, `witness_disk_health` may read `Unhealthy` for roughly five to fifteen minutes while metadata initialises. Shared-witness (vSphere 8.0 U2+) can read `degraded` for one stretched cluster when only that clusterâ€™s preferred site is isolated, even while the shared appliance is fineâ€”triage with per-cluster fault-domain view, not a blanket host rebuild. `connection_state=notResponding` on the poll line during the ESXi layer upgrade for the witness host is expected for the duration the vendor documents; use host-maintenance metadata or silence non-critical rows. A flaky collector that sometimes omits `last_witness_heartbeat` can trip `low` on heartbeat age without a true outageâ€”watch `_indextime` versus `_time` gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ vCenter 7.0 U2+ with `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/witness` (or a documented `pyVmomi` SOAP path) reachable from a collector with least-privilege.
â€˘ Python 3.9+ (vSphere Automation) or Windows PowerShell with VMware PowerCLI 13+, HEC to Splunk, TLS to vCenter 443, secrets in a vault.
â€˘ `lookups/vsan_witness_owner.csv` with `cluster, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, is_shared_witness` (optional flag as `0`/`1` strings).
â€˘ Baseline: one `vmware:vsan:witness` per stretched cluster per poll; fix duplicate cluster keys before you enable paging.

### **Step 1 â€” Validate the data feed**
Readiness checks before you schedule an alert. Prove that each poll from your Heavy Forwarder or automation account lands as newline JSON on `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:witness` with a stable `cluster` key that matches the lookup row you will use for ownership, and a `witness_host` that lines up with the vSphere Client inventory. Contract fields: `cluster`, `vcenter`, `witness_host` and optional camelCase; `witness_state` in {`connected`, `disconnected`, `notResponding`, `notFound`} before the SPL lowercases; `connection_state` alongside; integer `fault_domain_count` (expect three for normal stretch); `preferred_fd`, `secondary_fd`, `witness_fd` labels; `witness_disk_health` and `witness_host_health` strings; `quorum` in {`met`, `not_met`}; `vsan_object_health` with vendor-quoted values such as `Inaccessible` and the reduced-availability strings; `last_witness_heartbeat` as ISO-8601; on vSphere 8.0 U2+ shared-witness, `shared_witness_id` and multivalue `serving_clusters`. Sample you can `collect` to a dev index:

```
{"cluster":"sc-prod-emea","vcenter":"vc-emea.corp.local","witness_host":"vsan-witness-zurich.corp.local","witness_state":"connected","connection_state":"connected","fault_domain_count":3,"preferred_fd":"emea-pri","secondary_fd":"emea-sec","witness_fd":"emea-wit","witness_disk_health":"Healthy","witness_host_health":"Healthy","quorum":"met","vsan_object_health":"Healthy","last_witness_heartbeat":"2026-04-26T01:29:48Z"}
```

`props.conf` for `[vmware:vsan:witness]` should disable line merge, set JSON indexed extraction, raise `TRUNCATE` for long cluster lists, and point `TIME_PREFIX` at your heartbeat or poll time when the poller also emits `poll_utc`. Validate: `tstats` counts by `cluster, witness_host` should equal vCenter stretched clusters; NTP on collector versus vCenter should stay under thirty seconds; a camelCase-only fixture and a snake_case-only fixture should collapse to the same `severity_bucket` after `coalesce`. If you add `vmware:vsan:health` for dashboard overlays, do not double-count in this alert without a dedupe strategy.

### **Step 2 â€” Run the SPL**
Run the top-level `spl` from this use case in Search, save as a report, then promote to a scheduled alert. The `comment` macro is an audit hint only. The base search keeps `(index=vmware OR index=hci)`; standardise to one index in production unless you are migrating. Deduplication uses `stats` on `cluster, witness_host` so each pollâ€™s newest snapshot becomes one row. `hb_epoch` uses multiple `strptime` forms so a feed that flips between `Z` and numeric offsets still yields `heartbeat_age`. `severity_bucket` is ordered: cluster-partition and object-loss signals stay `critical` before heartbeat drift. The lookup enforces a service catalog row and a change freeze window. When `change_freeze_until` parses in the future, `freeze_active=1` and only non-critical buckets are downgraded to `ok`, so you can still get paged for quorum loss while silencing noisy `low` or `medium` during an approved long-haul link change. Fenced SPL (matches the `spl` field exactly):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.23 â€” vSAN Witness Appliance Health: (cluster, witness_host) severity from vmware:vsan:witness with heartbeat age, fault-domain count, change-freeze (non-critical). Boilerplate vsan:witness is not a real indexable sourcetype.")`
(index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:witness" earliest=-1h@h
| eval cluster=toString(coalesce(cluster, clusterName, cluster_name, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, ""))
| eval witness_host=toString(coalesce(witness_host, witnessHost, ""))
| eval witness_state_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_state, witnessState, ""))
| eval connection_state_raw=toString(coalesce(connection_state, connectionState, ""))
| eval fault_domain_count_raw=tonumber(toString(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, "-1")), 10)
| eval preferred_fd=toString(coalesce(preferred_fd, preferredFd, ""))
| eval secondary_fd=toString(coalesce(secondary_fd, secondaryFd, ""))
| eval witness_fd=toString(coalesce(witness_fd, witnessFd, ""))
| eval witness_disk_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_disk_health, witnessDiskHealth, ""))
| eval witness_host_raw=toString(coalesce(witness_host_health, witnessHostHealth, ""))
| eval quorum_raw=toString(coalesce(quorum, ""))
| eval voh_raw=toString(coalesce(vsan_object_health, vsanObjectHealth, ""))
| eval last_hb=toString(coalesce(last_witness_heartbeat, lastWitnessHeartbeat, ""))
| eval shared_witness_id=toString(coalesce(shared_witness_id, sharedWitnessId, ""))
| eval serving_clusters=coalesce(serving_clusters, servingClusters, "")
| stats max(_time) AS _time
    values(vcenter) AS vcenters
    latest(witness_state_raw) AS witness_state_l
    latest(connection_state_raw) AS connection_state_l
    latest(fault_domain_count_raw) AS fault_domain_count_l
    latest(preferred_fd) AS preferred_fd
    latest(secondary_fd) AS secondary_fd
    latest(witness_fd) AS witness_fd
    latest(witness_disk_raw) AS witness_disk_l
    latest(witness_host_raw) AS witness_host_l
    latest(quorum_raw) AS quorum_l
    latest(voh_raw) AS voh_l
    latest(last_hb) AS last_witness_heartbeat_l
    latest(shared_witness_id) AS shared_witness_id
    latest(serving_clusters) AS serving_clusters
  BY cluster, witness_host
| eval vcenter=if(mvcount(vcenters)=1, mvindex(vcenters, 0), mvjoin(vcenters, ", "))
| eval witness_state=lower(trim(witness_state_l))
| eval connection_state=lower(trim(connection_state_l))
| eval quorum=lower(trim(quorum_l))
| eval fault_domain_count=if(fault_domain_count_l>=0, fault_domain_count_l, null())
| eval witness_disk_health=lower(trim(witness_disk_l))
| eval witness_host_health=lower(trim(witness_host_l))
| eval vsan_object_health=trim(voh_l)
| eval last_witness_heartbeat=last_witness_heartbeat_l
| eval hb_epoch=coalesce(
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),
    strptime(last_witness_heartbeat, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N")
  )
| eval heartbeat_age=if(isnotnull(hb_epoch), now()-hb_epoch, null())
| eval crit_q=(quorum!="met")
| eval crit_w=(witness_state="disconnected" OR witness_state="notfound")
| eval crit_v=(vsan_object_health="Inaccessible")
| eval high_d=(witness_disk_health="unhealthy")
| eval high_hh=(witness_host_health="error")
| eval high_v=(vsan_object_health="Reduced-Availability-No-Rebuild")
| eval med_w=(witness_state="notresponding")
| eval med_hh=(witness_host_health="warning")
| eval med_v=(vsan_object_health="Reduced-Availability-With-Rebuild")
| eval low_hb=((isnotnull(heartbeat_age)) AND (heartbeat_age>300))
| eval low_fd=((isnotnull(fault_domain_count)) AND (fault_domain_count!=3))
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    (crit_q=1) OR (crit_w=1) OR (crit_v=1), "critical",
    (high_d=1) OR (high_hh=1) OR (high_v=1), "high",
    (med_w=1) OR (med_hh=1) OR (med_v=1), "medium",
    (low_hb=1) OR (low_fd=1), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| lookup vsan_witness_owner cluster OUTPUT business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, is_shared_witness
| eval freeze_active=if(
    isnotnull(change_freeze_until)
    AND (len(toString(change_freeze_until))>0)
    AND (now()<coalesce(strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(change_freeze_until), "%Y-%m-%d"), 0)),
    1, 0
  )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (severity_bucket!="critical"), "ok", severity_bucket)
| where severity_bucket!="ok"
| eval sev_order=case(
    severity_bucket="critical", 1,
    severity_bucket="high", 2,
    severity_bucket="medium", 3,
    severity_bucket="low", 4,
    1=1, 9
  )
| sort 0 sev_order, cluster, witness_host
| table _time, severity_bucket, sev_order, cluster, witness_host, vcenter, quorum, witness_state, connection_state, fault_domain_count, vsan_object_health, witness_disk_health, witness_host_health, heartbeat_age, last_witness_heartbeat, shared_witness_id, serving_clusters, business_service, owner_email, freeze_active, is_shared_witness, crit_q, crit_w, crit_v, high_d, high_hh, high_v, med_w, med_hh, med_v, low_hb, low_fd
```

### **Step 3 â€” Validate**
Good looks like: a steady poll with `heartbeat_age` well under 120 seconds for a 60s cadence, `fault_domain_count=3`, `quorum=met`, `vsan_object_health=Healthy` on the witness line, and event volume of roughly one per cluster per poll. In the vSphere Client, open the stretched cluster, confirm witness fault domain membership, and compare names to the Splunk `witness_host` and `cluster` fields. In parallel, the vSAN health UI and Skyline (if you subscribe) should agree with the poll-derived strings within one poll period; if the UI is red and Splunk is green, your poller is mapping the wrong API branch or the service account cannot read the witness MoRef. Expect zero alert rows in steady state; when you test, inject fixture JSON and confirm a single `critical` for `quorum=not_met` even under freeze. For shared-witness, list `serving_clusters` next to the vCenter inventory for each member stretch cluster. Document expected counts: number of `vmware:vsan:witness` events per five minutes should equal the number of stretched clusters times polls; a sudden half-count usually means a broken HEC token, not a silent witness removal.

### **Step 4 â€” Operationalize**
Save the search as `uc_19_2_23_vsan_witness_appliance_health` in your monitoring app. Schedule every five to ten minutes with `earliest=-1h@h` to match poller frequency. Pager: route `critical` to primary on-call for storage; route `high` to business hours unless a cluster row in `vsan_witness_owner` marks a revenue-facing database estate as always-on. Throttle on `cluster`+`witness_host` for at least thirty minutes so witness reboot storms during coordinated patching do not generate duplicate tickets. Add a second scheduled report with the `where` removed so leadership can see `ok` rows in a table when auditing posture. ServiceNow: map `owner_email` as assignment hint, pass `vcenter` and `witness_host` in the short description, and require a network bridge before anyone schedules hardware replacement. Keep `vsan_witness_owner.csv` in the same app as the search with Git-backed review; for shared-witness include `is_shared_witness=1` so the bridge template reminds responders that another stretch cluster can still be healthy. Dashboard row one: two single values for `critical` and `high`. Row two: timechart of ingest count for `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:witness` to see gaps. Row three: table of the alert output. Row four: sparkline of `heartbeat_age` for the top three clusters by event volume.

### **Step 5 â€” Tune & extend**
If `low` is too chatty, raise the heartbeat threshold from 300s only after you chart two weeks of poll delay; never silence `critical` for heartbeat alone. If `notresponding` is noisy for seconds-long TCP glitches, add a `streamstats` window or require two consecutive bad polls before you upgrade `medium` to a page. For lab clusters, tag `business_service=lab` in the lookup and point alerts to a dev channel. When VMware changes REST casing on a minor vCenter upgrade, re-run the coalesce test before production promotion. If you add Azure-style witness on Outposts, keep the same sourcetype so the SPL stays one report. If you need correlation with `vmware:vc:event` gaps, build a second alert, not a forced join, because event rate is not poll-shaped. Revisit the shared-witness case quarterly: a single `degraded` on one member cluster is often expected site isolation, not a hardware fault for the OVA. Governance: HEC token rotation, lookup edits, and threshold changes are separate change types with different approvers. Capacity: each poll line is a few kilobytes; 60s over hundreds of clusters is well under a typical daily license if you avoid duplicate copy to a second index. When business teams rename a cluster, update both vCenter and the pollerâ€™s cluster key before the lookup drifts, or every alert will look like a new object.
grandmaExplanation: We watch a tiny tie-breaker computer in a third place so the two main sites never both think they are in charge when the long link between them fails. Without that vote, the shared storage could act like two halves that both try to write the same data. We help you see trouble early so a blip in the line does not turn into a long hands-on clean-up later.
hardware: The supported vSAN witness is delivered as a VMware OVA with small, medium, and large form factors: small (about 8 GB RAM to the guest), medium (16 GB), and large (32 GB) depending on the stretched-cluster scale and vSphere track you purchased; the witness holds metadata for tie votes, not business VM data, so size your storage footprint to VMware guidance, not to guest workload counts. Stretched vSAN 6.5+ uses that dedicated witness in a third fault domain. vSAN ESA on 8.0 U1+ still relies on a witness for stretch designs; vSphere 8.0 U2 introduced a shared-witness OVA that can arbitrate for multiple stretched clusters, reducing duplicate third-site sprawl. Supported placement patterns include on-prem witness on general-purpose x86 (Dell PowerEdge, HPE ProLiant, Cisco UCS, Lenovo ThinkSystem) when you deploy the OVA to those clusters, a nested witness in Amazon EC2, vSAN on AWS Outposts, or VMware Cloud Directorâ€“hosted witness appliances where your subscription allows. When a hardware witness host is required instead of the OVA, treat it like a locked-down management cluster member with redundant power and out-of-band. Cross-check OEM HCI lines such as Dell VxRail for witness placement rules; those platforms still emit the same REST shape once polled.
